Dem & Mtn To Strike
Off Calendar Nov-09-2001 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEAR: Daniel H. Qualls, Esq. 782-3660 for pltf & opposing party; Richard D. Martland, Esq. 916/446-6752 for defts & moving parties Farmer's Insurance Exchange, Fire Insurance Exchange, Truck Insurance Exchange, Mid-Century Insurance Company, Farmer's Underwriter's Association;Jerry Roth, Esq. 512-4010 & Peter A. Detre, Esq. 512-4013 for deft & moving party Allstate Insurance Company; Bradley A. Benbrook, Esq. 916/329-9111 for defts & moving parties State Farm General Insurance Company and State Farm Fire and Casualty Company; Cheryl A. Williams, Esq. 213/243-4036 for deft & moving party 21st Century Insurance Company; James P. Kleinberg, Esq. & Patrick Weston, Esq. 650/849-4400 for deft & moving party Fireman's Fund Insurance Company; Steven R. Walker, Esq. 957-1800 for deft Old Republic Title; Bryan C. Crawley, Esq. 213/680-2800 (appearing via Courtcall)for deft Farmers Home Group (joinder).
RULING:
The Court announced its tentative ruling as follows:
Demurrer to first cause of action sustained with leave to amend; 2nd cause of action overruled.
Demurrer for improper joinder overruled.
SLAPP motion to strike denied.
Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as to the SLAPP motion & improper joinder and takes the demurrer under submission. Each to submit proposed orders by next Friday.
In addition, the Court grants 21st Century's motion to set aside default, no opposition.
